title: Unified persistent session adapter layer
summary: Implement a unified, ontology-driven persistent session adapter contract and registry so native adapters and plugin targets parse, list, resolve, resume, and expose sessions consistently across claude-code, codex, pi, gemini, opencode, and plugin-generated targets.
pluginGeneratedTargetRequirement: At least one plugin-generated target must be covered by fixture-backed parser/registry tests and Atlas/plugin-target projection tests.
compatibilityRequirements: Preserve native session IDs separately from unified IDs.
Preserve SessionManager resolveUnifiedId() and resolveNativeId() behavior unless a documented migration is approved.
Preserve existing AgentAdapter sessionDir(), listSessionFiles(), and parseSessionFile() as compatibility shims during migration.
Malformed or unreadable native session files must not break session listing.
Date-filtered fast listing must remain bounded and sorted.
Gateway /api/v1/sessions list/show/full behavior must remain stable except for documented new metadata fields.
CLI adapters sessions list/show/full/export/resume flows must work with fixture-backed adapters and must not require live provider credentials.
Atlas graph records must be tested inputs to runtime registry metadata, not untested documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: A concrete spec documents the unified session adapter contract, registry behavior, native ID handling, unified ID handling, parser responsibilities, and Atlas metadata inputs.
A representative fixture matrix exists before or alongside implementation for claude-code, codex, pi, gemini, opencode, and at least one plugin-generated target.
Shared registry/interface consumes structured Atlas SessionSemantics and PluginTarget metadata while preserving target-specific parsers for native format differences.
SessionManagerImpl, adapter codecs, and gateway session loading use the shared contract without prematurely breaking current adapter APIs.
Unit tests cover parser behavior from stable native session fixtures and legacy API compatibility.
E2e tests cover CLI adapters sessions list/show/full/export/resume and gateway /api/v1/sessions behavior.
Atlas projection tests prove metadata used by the session adapter matches catalog graph records.
Docs and tutorials explain the unified registry, compatibility shims, target coverage, and migration expectations.
Focused and broad quality gates pass or document precise environment-only unavailable evidence.

planningNotes.triageSummary: Add a unified, ontology-driven persistent session adapter layer for plugin targets and native adapters so session parsing/listing/resume behavior is consistent across claude-code, codex, pi, and related targets.
planningNotes.currentRootCause: Atlas has SessionSemantics and PluginTarget data while adapters runtime still has separate per-agent parser methods and gateway fallback parsing. There is no shared session-adapter interface/registry connecting ontology metadata to persistent session parsing behavior.
planningNotes.processShape: Flat phase list: feature requirements are known; first implementation phase is spec/contract and test-first planning, not open-ended forensics.
planningNotes.repoOverride: Use agent tasks for implementation, verification, review, and delivery; do not use kind: shell subtasks unless a later user explicitly asks for a shell-oriented workflow.
